pi_items.verified_value: Proceedings of the Institute of Radio Engineers
pi_items.source: Original 1952 Huffman paper title page shows 'PROCEEDINGS OF THE I.R.E.' - full official name is 'Proceedings of the Institute of Radio Engineers' as confirmed by IEEE Xplore, HathiTrust, and Computer History Museum archives

pi_items.verified_value: Communications of the Association for Computing Machinery
pi_items.source: McCarthy 1960 paper published in 'Communications of the Association for Computing Machinery, vol. 3' - confirmed by ACM Digital Library, Cambridge Core, and multiple academic sources. Modern shorthand is 'Communications of the ACM'
Dijkstra 1959 paper published in 'Numerische Mathematik, volume 1' - confirmed by Springer Nature journal archives and EUDML

pi_items.key: Mathematical Theory of Communication → journal name
pi_items.source: Shannon 1948 paper published in 'Bell System Technical Journal, Volume 27, pages 379-423' - confirmed by Wiley Online Library, Harvard Mathematics department, and Nokia Bell Labs archives
Church 1936 paper published in 'American Journal of Mathematics, Vol. 58' - confirmed by Cambridge Core Journal of Symbolic Logic, JSTOR, and Scientific Research Publishing references

privileged_info_summary: Three of the five journal name corrections have been verified as correct: Dijkstra's paper was published in Numerische Mathematik, Shannon's in Bell System Technical Journal, and Church's in American Journal of Mathematics. However, two metadata claims are contradicted: Huffman's paper appeared in Proceedings of the Institute of Radio Engineers (not just 'Proceedings of the IRE' as metadata claims), and McCarthy's paper appeared in Communications of the Association for Computing Machinery (not just 'Communications of the ACM'). While 'Proceedings of the IRE' and 'Communications of the ACM' are standard modern citation abbreviations, they technically contain unexpanded acronyms and do not represent the fully unabbreviated journal names.
